_comment: Hot-function map for the PPCG backend, keyed by source basename. fn: function(s) whose bodies get #pragma scop markers (Phase 0's per-program hot-kernel calls, SCOP_CLASSIFICATION.md). arrays: flat-pointer params ppcg_to_cu.py delinearizes into 2D VLA form (param -> row stride) -- pet cannot handle p[i*n+j] on a flat T* (bilinear subscript, undecidable extent; it silently emits zero kernels or hard-fails), but handles the equivalent (*p)[n] 2D form. Entries without a mode are bucket-A: PPCG output is the whole translation. mode=hybrid marks bucket-B Phase 3 targets: only the listed affine sub-kernels are GPU-ified and run_pipeline.py routes through the hybrid relay (PPCG partial + LLM glue-stitching). Either way the wrapper now fails loudly when no kernel comes out, so a bad entry falls through to the LLM path instead of shipping host-only code with a ppcg tag.

heat2d.c._why: currently falls through: step() computes a derived flat index (int i = y*m+x; u[i-1], u[i-m]) that textual delinearization can't rewrite -- needs region-level source normalization, same club as lu/qr/lbm

multigrid.c._why: recursive vcycle + per-level alloc stay host/LLM glue. residual() excluded (return inside would-be scop); restrict_fw() excluded (flat rc[i]=0 memset over nc*nc defeats textual delinearization)

rgf.c._why: per-block BxB GEMM is affine after delinearization (global B is a pet parameter); the sequential block sweep and pivoted mat_inv stay host/LLM glue. mat_sub() excluded (flat C[i] loop bounded by B*B -- a parameter product, non-affine)
